message:
Add ratelimit cooldown, templates helpers, and lint Disable tests

bd-8gkp7: 7 functions from 0% to 100% across 3 packages:
- ratelimit: RecordRateLimitWithCooldown, CooldownRemaining, IsInCooldown,
  ClearCooldown (78.0% → 92.0%)
- templates: TemplateSource.String, HasVariable (71.9% → 73.5%)
- lint: Disable (84.0% → 85.1%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-852e","title":"Task: Add --local and --ollama flags to spawn command","description":"## Overview
Extend the spawn command to support creating local LLM agent sessions.

## Deliverables
1. Update internal/cli/spawn.go:
   - Add --local=N flag: spawn N local agents
   - Add --ollama=N flag: alias for --local (explicit Ollama)
   - Add --local-model=<model> flag: specify model (default: codellama:latest)
   - Add --local-host=<url> flag: custom Ollama host

2. Flag combinations:
   - ntm spawn myproject --local=2 (2 local agents)
   - ntm spawn myproject --local=2 --local-model=deepseek-coder:33b
   - ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --local=2 (mixed fleet)

3. Validation:
   - Check Ollama is running before spawn
   - Check model exists (offer to pull if not)
   - Validate model name format

4. Session naming:
   - Local agents: myproject__ollama_1, myproject__ollama_2
   - Or: myproject__local_1 for generic naming
   - Include model name in agent metadata

## Implementation Notes
- Integrate with existing spawn infrastructure
- Reuse pane management code
- Store local agent config in session metadata

## Acceptance Criteria
- --local flag spawns local agent sessions
- Model selection works
- Mixed fleets function correctly
- Helpful errors when Ollama unavailable
- Unit tests for flag parsing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:53.838004739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:29:51.949269944Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T03:29:51.949207488Z","close_reason":"Implemented --local/--ollama spawn flags + preflight + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-852e","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-852e","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8a9h","title":"EPIC: Agent Output Archive with CASS Integration","description":"**Agent Output Archive with CASS Integration EPIC**

## What It Does
Archive all agent output for later retrieval and analysis, integrated with CASS for searchability.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Output lost when panes cleared
- After: Full history preserved and searchable
- Essential for debugging and learning

## Features
- Automatic output capture
- CASS indexing for search
- Retrieval by session, agent, or time range

## Success Criteria

1. All agent output captured automatically
2. Archives stored in configurable location
3. CASS integration indexes archives
4. Search by session, agent, keyword
5. Retrieval returns timestamped output
6. Archive rotation and retention policies
7. Unit tests for archival logic (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify archive creation and retrieval","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-8o977","title":"CAAM Integration: Coding Agent Account Manager for Rate Limit Recovery","description":"Integrate CAAM (coding_agent_account_manager) for instant account switching when agents hit rate limits.

## Project Location
/dp/coding_agent_account_manager (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point in multi-agent workflows:
- Claude Code: 5-minute to multi-hour cooldowns
- OpenAI Codex: Per-minute/per-day limits
- Gemini: Regional and tier-based limits

Current workaround: Wait, or manually switch browser profiles. This kills productivity.

## What CAAM Does

CAAM is an OAuth token vault with instant profile switching:
- Stores encrypted OAuth tokens for multiple accounts per provider
- Detects rate limit responses automatically
- Switches to next available account in <100ms
- Tracks usage per account to predict limits
- Supports: Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic API, etc.

### Key CAAM Commands
```bash
caam list                    # List all accounts
caam switch claude account2  # Switch to specific account
caam status                  # Show current account and usage
caam auto-rotate             # Enable automatic rotation on rate limit
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. Rate Limit Detection
NTM monitors agent output for rate limit signals:
- "Rate limit exceeded"
- HTTP 429 responses
- Provider-specific messages

### 2. Automatic Recovery
When rate limit detected:
1. Pause agent activity
2. Call: caam switch <provider> --next
3. Update environment variables for agent
4. Resume agent

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-switch-account: Manually trigger switch
- --robot-account-status: Get current account info
- --robot-accounts-list: List available accounts

### 4. Dashboard Integration
- Show current account per agent
- Show usage/limit percentages
- Alert when approaching 80% of limit

## Considerations
- Security: CAAM handles encryption, NTM just invokes CLI
- Atomicity: Account switch must be atomic (no half-switched state)
- Recovery: If CAAM fails, agent should continue with current account
- Latency: <100ms target for account switch","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:47:16.068201734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:40:41.871111517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:40:41.871056724Z","close_reason":"All CAAM integration tasks complete: detection, config, robot commands, auto-switching, dashboard widget","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8o977","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dgag","title":"E2E Tests: Score tracking and recommendations","description":"E2E tests for score tracking and recommendations.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Score Tracking
1. Record synthetic scores for multiple agents
2. Verify scores updated + persisted (JSONL)
3. Check trend window calculations

### Scenario 2: Recommendation Influence
1. Build score history per agent and task type
2. Request recommendations/assignments
3. Verify scores influence order (with explainable rationale)

### Scenario 3: Score Reset / Cold Start
1. Start with empty tracker
2. Verify recommendations return to default behavior
3. Add minimal samples → still gated
4. Add enough samples → scoring kicks in

## Logging
- Log input scores and computed ranking
- Log trend summary and sample counts
- Include session/agent/task/bead IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Score tracking verified across tasks
2. Recommendation order matches scores (when gated)
3. Score persistence works correctly
4. Reset/cold-start behavior tested
5. Test logs include score values
6. Test duration under 5 minutes

 {"id":"bd-dkxa0","title":"Task: Implement ContextPack types and project brief structure","description":"Define core types for the context pack system.

## Types
```go
// internal/ensemble/context.go
type ContextPack struct {
    Hash            string         // For cache keying
    GeneratedAt     time.Time
    ProjectBrief    *ProjectBrief
    UserContext     *UserContext
    TokenEstimate   int            // Estimated tokens when injected
}

type ProjectBrief struct {
    Name            string
    Description     string         // From README
    Languages       []string       // Detected
    Frameworks      []string       // From dependencies
    Structure       *ProjectStructure
    RecentActivity  []CommitSummary
    OpenIssues      int            // From .beads
}

type ProjectStructure struct {
    EntryPoints     []string
    CorePackages    []string
    TestCoverage    float64
    TotalFiles      int
    TotalLines      int
}

type UserContext struct {
    ProblemStatement string
    FocusAreas       []string
    Constraints      []string
    Stakeholders     []string
}
```

 {"id":"bd-dz3r","title":"EPIC: Automatic File Reservation via Agent Mail","description":"**IDEA #5 - INVISIBLE SAFETY NET (87% confidence)**

## What It Does
When NTM detects an agent is editing a file (from pane output analysis), it automatically reserves that file in Agent Mail, preventing other agents from conflicting edits.

## Why This Matters
- Conflicts between agents are a major pain point
- Currently: Users must remember to manually reserve files
- After: It just works; conflicts are prevented automatically
- Users don't even notice until they get a conflict warning (which is now rare)

## How It Works
Detection triggers:
1. Tool use detection: Parse pane output for tool calls that write files
   - Claude: Edit, Write, NotebookEdit tool names
   - Codex: write_file, edit_file patterns
   - Gemini: Similar patterns
2. Path extraction: Extract file paths from tool calls
3. Automatic reservation: Call Agent Mail file_reservation_paths
4. TTL management: Extend reservation while agent continues editing; release on idle

## Flywheel Alignment
- Makes Agent Mail's file reservation feature actually get used
- Most users don't manually reserve files; this makes it automatic
- Increases Agent Mail's value proposition significantly

## Technical Implementation
- New file: internal/watcher/file_reservation.go
- FileReservationWatcher struct with background monitoring
- Detection patterns extended from robot/patterns.go
- Config under [file_reservation] section
- Conflict notification to dashboard/alerts

## Dependencies
Depends on: ntm init (for Agent Mail registration)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

The FileReservationWatcher is now integrated into the dashboard lifecycle and automatically starts when Agent Mail is available and file_reservation.enabled=true in config.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dz3r","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ee3e6","title":"LimitDetector: Background monitoring goroutines","description":"## LimitDetector: Background Monitoring Goroutines

### Purpose
Implement the background monitoring loop that continuously checks panes for limit patterns.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Monitoring Loop:**
```go
// monitorPane runs the monitoring loop for a single pane.
func (d *LimitDetector) monitorPane(ctx context.Context, sessionPane, agentType string) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(d.checkInterval) // Default 5s
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            log.Info("[LimitDetector] monitor_stopped", "session_pane", sessionPane)
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            event, err := d.checkOnce(sessionPane, agentType)
            if err != nil {
                log.Warn("[LimitDetector] check_failed", 
                    "session_pane", sessionPane, "error", err)
                continue
            }
            if event != nil {
                // Send event to channel (non-blocking with timeout)
                select {
                case d.eventChan <- *event:
                    log.Info("[LimitDetector] limit_event_emitted", 
                        "session_pane", sessionPane, "pattern", event.Pattern)
                case <-time.After(1 * time.Second):
                    log.Warn("[LimitDetector] event_channel_full", 
                        "session_pane", sessionPane)
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

// checkOnce performs a single check of a pane.
func (d *LimitDetector) checkOnce(sessionPane, agentType string) (*LimitEvent, error) {
    output, err := d.capturePaneOutput(sessionPane, 50)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    matched, pattern := d.matcher.Match(agentType, output)
    if !matched {
        return nil, nil
    }
    
    // Record in tracker
    d.Tracker.RecordRateLimit(agentType, "monitor")
    
    return &LimitEvent{
        SessionPane: sessionPane,
        AgentType:   agentType,
        Pattern:     pattern,
        RawOutput:   output,
        DetectedAt:  time.Now(),
    }, nil
}
```

**Start All Panes:**
```go
// Start begins monitoring all panes in the swarm plan.
func (d *LimitDetector) Start(ctx context.Context, plan *SwarmPlan) error {
    d.ctx, d.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
    
    for _, session := range plan.Sessions {
        for _, pane := range session.Panes {
            sessionPane := fmt.Sprintf("%s:1.%d", session.Name, pane.Index)
            go d.monitorPane(d.ctx, sessionPane, session.AgentType)
            log.Info("[LimitDetector] monitor_started", 
                "session_pane", sessionPane, "agent_type", session.AgentType)
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

### Configuration
```go
type LimitDetectorConfig struct {
    CheckInterval   time.Duration // Default: 5s
    EventBufferSize int           // Default: 100
    CaptureLines    int           // Default: 50
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log monitor start: `[LimitDetector] monitor_started session_pane=%s agent_type=%s interval=%v`
- Log check: `[LimitDetector] check_complete session_pane=%s matched=%t` (debug)
- Log event emit: `[LimitDetector] limit_event_emitted session_pane=%s pattern=%s`
- Log stop: `[LimitDetector] monitor_stopped session_pane=%s`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Background goroutine per pane
- [ ] Configurable check interval
- [ ] Non-blocking event emission
- [ ] Graceful shutdown via context cancellation
- [ ] Integration with RateLimitTracker","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:03.809293623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:36:37.911529085Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:36:37.910703279Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ee3e6","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ee3e6","depends_on_id":"bd-3no20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
